wiki_catalogs.name: Project Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the 导弹飞行棋 project overview section. Explain the project's purpose as a multiplayer online flying chess game with military-themed combat mechanics, its core value proposition, and target audience. Document the key features including anti-air combat, air-to-air missile duels, anti-radar systems, and cruise missile attacks. Describe the technology stack (TypeScript, Socket.IO, React 18) and architectural approach. Include both conceptual overviews for beginners and technical highlights for experienced developers. Use terminology consistent with the codebase. Provide practical examples demonstrating the game's unique mechanics and multiplayer capabilities.

wiki_catalogs.name: React Application Structure
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the React 18 application structure of 导弹飞行棋. Document the component hierarchy in App.tsx, screen-based routing system, and main entry point configuration. Explain the Vite build configuration, HTML template setup, and development server configuration. Cover component organization patterns, import strategies, and module resolution. Detail the screen management system that switches between lobby, room, and game views. Include React best practices, component composition patterns, and performance optimization techniques used in the application.

wiki_catalogs.name: Core Gameplay Rules
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the core gameplay mechanics of 导弹飞行棋. Document the dice-based movement system including takeoff procedures, plane movement logic, and collision detection. Explain the jump chain mechanics on same-color cells, shortcut traversal system, and stacking rules. Detail the turn-based state machine with phase transitions, player actions, and game progression. Cover the board layout, cell types, and positional calculations. Include mathematical models for movement steps, bounce mechanics, and positional validation. Provide examples of common scenarios like takeoff decisions, movement choices, and legal move validation.

wiki_catalogs.name: Server Infrastructure
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive server infrastructure documentation for the 导弹飞行棋 Socket.IO server implementation. Explain the static file serving capabilities, server lifecycle management, and environment-specific configurations. Cover the relationship between server entry point and game engine initialization. Detail the TypeScript compilation setup, module resolution, and build optimization strategies. Include deployment considerations, port configuration, and security headers. Address server monitoring, logging strategies, and error handling at the infrastructure level.

Cover the user input validation, room code formatting, and error prevention mechanisms. Include the component's visual design elements, responsive layout considerations, and accessibility features. Provide examples of component usage and integration patterns with the Socket.IO client.

wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive getting started documentation for 导弹飞行棋. Address browser compatibility requirements and provide guidance for testing with multiple browser tabs/windows.

wiki_overview.content:
<blog>

# 防控作战飞行棋 - Multiplayer Strategy Board Game

## Project Introduction

**防控作战飞行棋** (Anti-Defense Combat Flying Chess) is a sophisticated multiplayer strategy board game that combines elements of traditional flying chess with military-themed gameplay mechanics. This project represents a modern, real-time implementation of a complex board game with advanced networking capabilities, comprehensive game state management, and rich interactive features.

### Purpose Statement
The game provides an immersive multiplayer experience where 2-4 players compete as military aircraft controlling squadrons against each other on a strategic board layout. Players navigate their planes through different phases including takeoff, movement, combat encounters, and special cell interactions while answering questions to gain advantages.

### Core Goals and Objectives
- **Real-time Multiplayer Experience**: Enable simultaneous play across multiple players with authoritative game state management
- **Complex Strategy Mechanics**: Implement sophisticated flight mechanics including takeoff sequences, collision detection, and tactical combat
- **Educational Integration**: Incorporate question-and-answer mechanics that enhance learning while maintaining competitive gameplay
- **Cross-platform Accessibility**: Provide both web-based and socket-based connectivity for seamless multiplayer experiences

### Target Audience
- Strategy game enthusiasts seeking complex tactical gameplay
- Educational institutions looking for interactive learning tools
- Multiplayer gaming communities interested in competitive board games
- Developers studying real-time game architecture and state synchronization

## Technical Architecture

The project follows a modern monorepo architecture with clear separation of concerns across three main domains:

```mermaid
graph TD
    A[Client Web App] --> B[Socket.IO Server]
    B --> C[Game Engine]
    B --> D[Room Management]
    C --> E[Game Rules Engine]
    C --> F[Combat System]
    C --> G[Deck Management]
    D --> H[Player Registry]
    D --> I[Game State]
    J[Shared Types] --> A
    J --> B
    J --> C
    K[Static Assets] --> A
    L[Question Data] --> C
```

### Component Breakdown

**Frontend Layer (React)**
- Real-time state synchronization through Socket.IO
- Component-based UI architecture with Zustand for state management
- Responsive design supporting various screen sizes
- Interactive board visualization with animated plane movements

**Backend Layer (Node.js)**
- Socket.IO server handling real-time communication
- Game engine managing complex state transitions
- Room management system coordinating multiplayer sessions
- Authoritative state enforcement preventing cheating

**Shared Layer (TypeScript)**
- Type-safe communication protocols
- Cross-platform type definitions
- Validation schemas for data integrity
- Protocol definitions for client-server interaction

### Design Patterns

**1. Monorepo Architecture**
- Clear separation between frontend, backend, and shared components
- Consistent TypeScript configuration across all packages
- Workspace management for efficient development workflows

**2. Event-Driven Communication**
- Socket.IO for real-time bidirectional communication
- Message-based protocol for all client-server interactions
- Centralized event handling with validation layers

**3. State Machine Pattern**
- Authoritative game state management
- Clear phase transitions and validation rules
- Predictable state progression with rollback capabilities

**4. Repository Pattern**
- RoomRegistry managing game sessions
- Player management with persistent identities
- GameEngine encapsulating business logic

## Key Implementation

### Main Entry Points

**Server Entry Point** (`server/src/index.ts`)
The server acts as both a WebSocket server and static file server, serving the React application in production mode while handling real-time game state synchronization.

**Client Entry Point** (`web/src/main.tsx`)
The React application initializes with Socket.IO client connection and provides a responsive interface for game interaction.

**Shared Types** (`shared/src/types.ts`)
Defines the complete type system shared between client and server, ensuring type safety across all communication layers.

### Core Modules

**Game Engine** (`server/src/game/engine.ts`)
The heart of the game logic, implementing complex flight mechanics including:
- Plane movement with collision detection
- Tactical combat system with AAM/AIM exchanges
- Special cell interactions (missile factories, radar stations, libraries)
- Card-based reward and punishment systems
- Question-and-answer integration for educational gameplay

**Room Management** (`server/src/rooms.ts`)
Manages multiplayer sessions with:
- Player registration and authentication
- Seat assignment and game initialization
- Game state broadcasting to connected clients
- Session persistence and cleanup

**Network Handlers** (`server/src/net/handlers.ts`)
Handles all client-server communication with:
- Zod-based payload validation
- Socket.IO event routing
- Error handling and recovery mechanisms
- Real-time state synchronization

**Client State Management** (`web/src/state/store.ts`)
Implements centralized state management using Zustand for:
- Real-time game state updates
- User interface state coordination
- Event logging and notification systems
- Local caching of game events

### Configuration Approach

**Workspace Configuration** (`package.json`)
Monorepo setup with npm workspaces enabling:
- Shared development dependencies
- Cross-package builds and deployments
- Concurrent development processes

**TypeScript Configuration** (`tsconfig.base.json`)
Centralized TypeScript settings for:
- Strict type checking across all packages
- Modular compilation strategy
- Path mapping for clean imports

**Build Configuration** (`web/vite.config.ts`)
Vite-powered frontend build with:
- Hot module replacement for development
- Optimized production builds
- Asset optimization and bundling

### External Dependencies

**Core Runtime Dependencies**
- `socket.io`: Real-time bidirectional event-based communication
- `zustand`: Lightweight state management solution
- `nanoid`: Cryptographically secure ID generation
- `zod`: Runtime type validation and schema definition

**Development Dependencies**
- `typescript`: Type safety and enhanced developer experience
- `vite`: Fast frontend build toolchain
- `concurrently`: Parallel process execution for development

### Integration Points

**Socket.IO Protocol** (`shared/src/protocol.ts`)
Defines the complete communication contract between client and server, including:
- Event naming conventions and payload structures
- Validation schemas for all message types
- Error handling and status reporting mechanisms

**Game State Synchronization**
Real-time state broadcasting ensures all clients receive consistent game updates, with the server acting as the authoritative source of truth.

### Component Relationship Diagrams

```mermaid
graph LR
    A[Client Store] --> B[Socket.IO Client]
    B --> C[Socket.IO Server]
    C --> D[Room Registry]
    D --> E[Game Engine]
    E --> F[Board Manager]
    E --> G[Combat System]
    E --> H[Deck System]
    C --> I[Event Handlers]
    I --> J[State Broadcast]
    J --> A
```

## Key Features

### Functionality Overview

**Multiplayer Gameplay**
- Real-time competitive play with 2-4 players
- Turn-based strategy with simultaneous actions
- Dynamic seat assignment and ready-state management
- Spectator-friendly room state visualization

**Advanced Flight Mechanics**
- Complex plane movement with collision detection
- Strategic takeoff sequences and landing procedures
- Tactical shortcuts and jump chains for positioning
- Perched plane mechanics for stacking strategies

**Tactical Combat System**
- AAM (Air-to-Air Missile) and SAM (Surface-to-Air Missile) exchanges
- AIM (Air-to-Ground) and ARM (Anti-Radar Missile) tactical options
- Cruise missile targeting for takeoff and landing strips
- Shield mechanics for defensive positioning

**Educational Integration**
- Question-and-answer system integrated into gameplay
- Knowledge-based rewards and penalties
- Adaptive difficulty through question categories
- Learning opportunities during combat resolution

### Implementation Highlights

**Game State Management** (`server/src/game/engine.ts`)
Comprehensive state machine implementation with:
- 16+ distinct game phases and transitions
- Authoritative state enforcement preventing illegal moves
- Event-driven logging system for game history
- Automatic game termination conditions

**Real-time Communication** (`server/src/net/handlers.ts`)
Robust networking layer with:
- Zod-based payload validation for all inputs
- Socket.IO room-based broadcasting
- Error propagation and recovery mechanisms
- Connection state management for player persistence

**Client-Side State** (`web/src/state/store.ts`)
Efficient client-side state management featuring:
- Real-time synchronization with server updates
- Local event caching for offline resilience
- UI state coordination across multiple components
- Error boundary handling and user feedback

### Feature Architecture Diagrams

```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> Lobby
    Lobby --> Room: Join/Create
    Room --> Game: Start
    Game --> AwaitRoll: Player Turn
    AwaitRoll --> AwaitTakeoffChoice: Takeoff Available
    AwaitRoll --> AwaitMoveChoice: Movement Available
    AwaitTakeoffChoice --> AwaitMoveChoice: Takeoff Selected
    AwaitMoveChoice --> Resolving: Move Completed
    Resolving --> AwaitRoll: Next Player
    Resolving --> AwaitCombat: Combat Encounter
    Resolving --> AwaitQA: Library Interaction
    AwaitCombat --> Resolving: Combat Resolved
    AwaitQA --> Resolving: Question Answered
    AwaitRoll --> GameOver: Victory Condition
    GameOver --> [*]
```

**Performance Considerations**
- Efficient state serialization for network transmission
- Client-side prediction for responsive interactions
- Server-side authoritative validation for security
- Memory-efficient event logging with automatic pruning

**Scalability Features**
- Stateless room management for horizontal scaling
- Event-driven architecture supporting multiple concurrent games
- Modular design enabling feature expansion
- Load balancing support through stateless server design

---

**Sources:**
- [package.json](package.json)
- [server/src/index.ts](server/src/index.ts)
- [web/src/App.tsx](web/src/App.tsx)
- [server/src/game/engine.ts](server/src/game/engine.ts)
- [shared/src/types.ts](shared/src/types.ts)
- [web/src/state/store.ts](web/src/state/store.ts)
- [server/src/net/handlers.ts](server/src/net/handlers.ts)
- [web/src/ui/Game.tsx](web/src/ui/Game.tsx)
- [server/package.json](server/package.json)
- [web/package.json](web/package.json)
- [shared/package.json](shared/package.json)
- [shared/src/protocol.ts](shared/src/protocol.ts)
- [server/src/rooms.ts](server/src/rooms.ts)
- [server/src/game/board.ts](server/src/game/board.ts)

</blog>
